steps.title: Identify what the quotation must show
steps.content: The claim focuses on May's puzzlement about Newland's reluctance. The quotation must directly show this puzzlement — that May doesn't understand his behavior.

steps.title: Find the quotation that demonstrates May's puzzlement
steps.content:
Choice C shows exactly this: “May herself could not understand [Newland's] obscure reluctance.” The phrase “could not understand” directly expresses her puzzlement about his behavior.

Choices A, B, and
  • D lack this: A is merely a factual detail about the archery club
  • B characterizes May's general nature without addressing her current confusion
  • D describes Newland's internal excitement rather than May's inability to grasp his reluctance
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
